SummaryAccess of resource using incompatible type ('type confusion') in Composite Image File System Driver allows an authorized attacker to disclose information locally.
CategoryIS
SourceMicrosoft MSRC
Published2026-07-14T14:00:00Z
TitleCVE-2026-50300 Windows DWM Core Library Information Disclosure Vulnerability
URLhttps://msrc.microsoft.com/update-guide/vulnerability/CVE-2026-50300
SummaryInteger underflow (wrap or wraparound) in Windows Kernel allows an authorized attacker to disclose information locally.
CategoryIS
SourceMicrosoft MSRC
Published2026-07-14T14:00:00Z
TitleCVE-2026-50303 Windows Key Guard Security Feature Bypass Vulnerability
URLhttps://msrc.microsoft.com/update-guide/vulnerability/CVE-2026-50303
SummaryUse of a cryptographic primitive with a risky implementation in Windows Key Guard allows an authorized attacker to bypass a security feature locally.
CategoryIS
SourceMicrosoft MSRC
Published2026-07-14T14:00:00Z

URLhttps://msrc.microsoft.com/update-guide/vulnerability/CVE-2026-50364

SourceMicrosoft MSRC
Published2026-07-14T14:00:00Z

TitleSuspended Counterparty Program
URLhttps://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2026/07/13/2026-14036/suspended-counterparty-program
SummaryThe Federal Housing Finance Agency (FHFA) is proposing to amend its Suspended Counterparty Program (SCP) regulation by removing the term "reputational harm." This amendment would eliminate redundancy and affirm that FHFA's supervision of counterparty risk is based on material and measurable risks.

SourceFederal Register

SummaryImproper access control in Microsoft Edge (Chromium-based) allows an unauthorized attacker to bypass a security feature over a network.

TitleIRS simplifies penalty relief, introduces automatic process for eligible taxpayers
URLhttps://www.irs.gov/newsroom/irs-simplifies-penalty-relief-introduces-automatic-process-for-eligible-taxpayers
SummaryIR-2026-83, July 8, 2026 — The Internal Revenue Service today announced a new automatic process to provide penalty relief for taxpayers with a history of filing and paying on time, reducing the need for them to request assistance.

TitleFed requests comment on changes to AML program rules
URLhttps://www.texasbankers.com/fed-requests-comment-on-changes-to-aml-program-rules/
SummaryThe Federal Reserve requests public comments on a proposal to amend its requirements for banks to maintain anti-money laundering programs. The amendments are intended to align with changes to anti-money laundering program requirements proposed separately by four other agencies. Among other changes, the proposal would require banks to allocate anti-money laundering resources ba…

URLhttps://www.irs.gov/newsroom/irs-security-summit-launch-summer-series-to-help-tax-pros-protect-clients-from-identity-theft
SummaryIR-2026-81, July 7, 2026 — The IRS and Security Summit partners today launched the summer “Protect Your Clients; Protect Yourself” campaign, a five-week series highlighting practical steps tax professionals can take to protect client data, their businesses, and themselves from evolving tax-related identity theft threats.

URLhttps://msrc.microsoft.com/update-guide/vulnerability/CVE-2026-55945

CategoryIS
SourceMicrosoft MSRC
Published2026-07-03T14:00:00Z
TitleCVE-2026-56645 Microsoft Edge (Chromium-based) Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
URLhttps://msrc.microsoft.com/update-guide/vulnerability/CVE-2026-56645
SummaryHeap-based buffer overflow in Microsoft Edge (Chromium-based) allows an unauthorized attacker to execute code over a network.
CategoryIS
SourceMicrosoft MSRC
Published2026-07-03T14:00:00Z
TitleCVE-2026-57975 Microsoft Edge (Chromium-based) Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
URLhttps://msrc.microsoft.com/update-guide/vulnerability/CVE-2026-57975

SourceMicrosoft MSRC
Published2026-07-03T14:00:00Z

TitlePress Release: FDIC Issues List of Banks Examined for CRA Compliance
URLhttps://content.govdelivery.com/accounts/USFDIC/bulletins/41e895d
SummaryPRESS RELEASE | JULY 2, 2026 FDIC Issues List of Banks Examined for CRA Compliance WASHINGTON — The Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) today issued its list of state nonmember banks recently evaluated for compliance with the Community Reinvestment Act (CRA). The list covers evaluation ratings that the FDIC assigned to institutions in April 2026. The CRA is a 1977 law…
